basis: to be eager/jealous/envy — burning desire; affect with a volitional drive toward an object
zealot/zealous — one characterised by burning eager devotion
to seek/strive after — purposeful pursuit; goal-directed willing, not the act of physical searching alone
to count/regard/consider — a mental act of estimation/judgement
gladly — disposition of pleasure/gladness in action
most gladly — superlative of pleasure/gladness
pleasure/passions — felt pleasure/desire
half-dead — a physical/bodily state, not an inner capacity
peaceful/quiet — a state, not a faculty in operation
to be quiet/cease — a behavioural state of stillness
quietness — a state
quiet — descriptive state
to be astonished/amazed — felt wonder
amazement/wonder — felt astonishment
take heart/be of good courage — courage is an affective-volitional resolve, not perception
heart/courage — courage as affective-volitional resolve
marvel/wonder — felt wonder
to marvel/be astonished — felt wonder
will/desire — the faculty of willing
to will/desire/want — the act of willing
reverence for God/godliness — reverent devotion (affect toward God)
godly/worshiper — characterised by reverent devotion
to press/afflict — external pressure/affliction (an event done to one), not an inner faculty
tribulation/affliction — external circumstance/pressure, not an inner faculty
religion/worship — an institution/practice, not an inner faculty per se
to be angry/quarrel — anger (affect)
to become furious/angry — anger (affect)
to propitiate/be merciful — a cultic/relational ACT of atonement, not an inner faculty (mercy here is the act)
propitiation — a thing/means of atonement
mercy seat/propitiation — a thing/place
propitious/merciful/gracious — disposition of mercy (affective leaning)
to desire/long for — felt longing
like-minded/of same soul — a relational state of agreement, not itself a faculty
strong/mighty — physical/power state, not an inner faculty
strength/might — power, not an inner faculty
to be strong/able — capacity for power/ability, not an inner faculty
to clean/cleanse — an act producing a clean state
cleansing/purification — a process/state
clean/pure — a state (incl. moral purity), not a faculty in operation despite cur conscience/moral tag
new — a state/quality
evil/malice/wickedness — moral quality measured against a standard; carries malice (also borders affect)
to curse/speak evil — a speech ACT
to do evil/harm — a physical/transfer act of harm
wrongdoer/evildoer — a person/agent, not a faculty
to harm/mistreat — an act of harm done to another, not an inner faculty
to call/invite — a summoning/speech act
heart — the central inner-being SEAT; operates the whole range of faculties
heart-knower — one who knows/perceives the heart (knowing capacity, of God)
to conquer/overcome — striving/accomplishing act
to condemn — judgement against a standard
to enslave — an act/transfer, not an inner faculty
to oppress — an act done to others
to put to shame/dishonor — induces shame (affect/conscience-related felt state)
to condemn/judge — judgement against a standard
condemnation — verdict against a standard
to master/domineer — an act of dominating, not an inner faculty
to slander/speak against — a speech act
slander — speech act/record
slanderer — a person/agent
reconciliation — relational restoration (a state/result of an act)
to reconcile — relational restoration act
to oppress/greatly distress — induces felt distress (and act of oppression)
to be cunning/deal shrewdly — cunning is misdirected cognition/cleverness
sensual desire/passions draw — felt sensual desire
mutilation/(of flesh) — a physical thing/act
to kiss (fervently) — an affectionate act expressing affect
idolatrous/full of idols — a state/quality, not a faculty
to rule/exercise authority — an act of power, not an inner faculty
to prevail/overpower — an act of power
to boast/glory — affective self-exaltation/exulting
boasting/pride — affective exulting
boasting/pride — affective exulting
to gain/win — a transfer/acquisition act
to weep/mourn — expression of grief (affect)
weeping — grief (affect)
calling/condition — a status/summons, not a faculty
called — a status, not a faculty
fellowship/participation/sharing — relational bond/act
partner/participant — a person/role
to mourn/wail (beat breast) — expression of grief (affect)
to cry out — a vocal act
mighty — a power quality
power/dominion/might — power, not an inner faculty
greater/better/superior — a comparative quality
to judge/decide — discriminating judgement against a standard (cognition + moral evaluation; volition secondary)
judgment — verdict against a standard
to lord over/rule — an act of dominion
lord/master — a person/role/title
lordship/dominion/authority — a domain/status of power
worship/service/ritual duties — a cultic act/practice
to serve/worship — a cultic act/practice
ministry/service/worship — a service act/office
rich/sumptuous (delicacies) — a material quality
to reckon/count/consider/regard — mental act of accounting/reasoning
rational/reasonable (spiritual worship) — pertaining to reason
thought/argument/reasoning — product of cognition
quarrel about words — a verbal dispute act
word/speech/reason — expressed reason/discourse (also a thing); reason faculty engaged
reviling/slander — a speech act
to grieve/be sorrowful — grief (affect)
to rave/be out of one's mind — disordered cognition
blessedness/blessing — a state of felt happiness/blessing
to be patient/long-suffering — patient endurance is affective restraint with volitional resolve
patience/longsuffering — affective restraint with volitional resolve
patiently — patient disposition
insanity/madness — disordered cognition
to translate/mean — an interpretive cognitive act
anxiety/care — anxious concern (affect) with anxious thought (cognition)
to be anxious/worry/care — anxious concern (affect+cognition)
arbiter/divider — a role/person
to change one's mind — a cognitive-volitional reversal
to share/impart/contribute — a transfer/giving act
to regret/repent — felt remorse (affect) with reconsideration (cognition); borders conscience
to transform/transfigure — a change of form act/process
to repent — a change of mind/purpose (cognition + volitional turning)
repentance — change of mind/purpose
to deal gently/moderate one's feelings — affective restraint
to stain/defile — an act producing a defiled state
defilement (defiling passion) — a state/result
to remember/be mindful — memory
to remember/call to mind — memory
remembrance/mention — memory
remembrance/recall — memory
to remember/keep in mind — memory
to defile/soil — an act producing a defiled state
defilement — a state/result
to form/shape — a forming act (of form, not creative ideation)
to make/become foolish — defective cognition
foolishness/folly — defective cognition
foolish/fool — defective cognition; in biblical usage moral folly judged against wisdom standard
to put to death/deaden — a physical/mortifying act
kidneys/reins (mind) — inner-being SEAT of deep affections and thought
sober-minded/temperate — clear-headed self-governed cognition
to be sober/clear-minded — alert clear-headed cognition
mind/thought/design — product/faculty of cognition
to be sick (unhealthy craving) — a diseased state
illness/disease — a physical state
mind/understanding — the faculty of cognition and moral discernment
to be in anguish/sorrow — felt anguish (affect)
anguish/pangs — felt anguish (affect)
mourning — grief (affect)
household servant/slave — a person/role
to build/build up/edify — a building act (literal/figurative), not itself a faculty
building/edification — a structure/result of building
to have compassion/pity — felt compassion (affect)
compassion/mercies — felt compassion (affect)
compassionate/merciful — disposition of compassion (affect)
to delay/hesitate/be reluctant — a volitional holding back
slothful/lazy — failure of willed diligence (volition/conscientiousness)
of little faith — weak trust (faith as affect+commitment)
fainthearted/discouraged — affective low state of soul
like-minded/of one mind — a relational state of agreement
to have joy/benefit (of) — felt benefit/joy
wrath/anger — anger (affect); divine wrath carries judicial moral evaluation
to be angry/enraged — anger (affect)
quick-tempered — disposition to anger (affect)
to aspire/long for/reach after — felt longing reaching toward an object (affect+volition)
lust/passion/craving — felt desire (affect)
holy/devout/sacred — a moral-religious state/quality, not a faculty in operation
holiness — a state/quality
almighty — a title/attribute of power
to transgress/break — an act against a standard (record), not itself a faculty
transgression — a deed/record against law
transgressor — a person/agent
to disgrace/expose to contempt — an act of public shaming done to another
to make jealous/provoke to jealousy — induces jealousy (affect)
to exhort/comfort/urge — comfort engages affect; exhortation engages persuasion (cognition)
comfort/encouragement/exhortation — felt comfort/encouragement
helper/advocate/counsellor — a role/person, not an inner faculty
disobedience (failure to hear/heed) — failure of attentive hearing (perception) and submission (volition)
to refuse to hear/ignore — failure of attentive hearing (perception) and willed heeding (volition)
comfort/consolation — felt comfort
lawlessness/transgression — a deed/record against law
to rebel/provoke — willed rebellion against
rebellion/provocation — willed rebellion
trespass/transgression — a deed/record against a standard
to be insane/beside oneself — disordered cognition
madness/insanity — disordered cognition
comfort/solace — felt comfort
to provoke/irritate — induced irritation/anger (affect)
sharp disagreement/stirring up — provoked feeling (affect); note also a stimulus to good deeds
to anger/provoke to wrath — anger (affect)
anger/exasperation — anger (affect)
boldness/confidence — courageous confident disposition (affect+volition)
to speak/act boldly — courageous confident expression
to persuade/be convinced/trust — persuasion engages cognition; being convinced yields confident trust (volition)
test/trial/experience — a trial event/thing, not a faculty
to tempt — solicitation to evil, engaging the will (testing of volition)
to test/prove — a proving act directed at one's resolve/will
to try/attempt — attempting engages will; testing of resolve
temptation — a trial/solicitation event, not a faculty
testing/trial — a trial event/circumstance
to try/attempt — willed attempt
poor/needy — a material/social state
to mourn/grieve — grief (affect)
grief/mourning — grief (affect)
confidence/trust — confident reliance (affect+volition)
deeply sorrowful — grief (affect)
to embitter/be harsh — bitterness of feeling (affect); also literal 'make bitter'
bitterness — bitter resentful feeling (affect)
bitter (taste/salt water) — primarily a sensory quality; figurative bitterness secondary
bitterly (wept bitterly) — bitter feeling (affect)
to believe/trust in — trust as both felt reliance (affect) and commitment (volition)
faith — trust/reliance (affect+volition); conscience secondary
faithfulness — steadfast reliability (willed commitment; conscience-integrity)
faithful/trustworthy — reliable commitment and integrity
to be convinced/assured — settled trust (affect+volition)
greedy/covetous person — characterised by covetous desire (affect)
greed/covetousness — covetous desire (affect)
rich — a material state
richly/abundantly — a manner/quantity
to be rich/gain wealth — a material state/acquisition
to enrich/make rich — a transfer/material act
riches/wealth — a thing/material abundance
spirit/breath — the inner-being SEAT/life-principle; operates the inner faculties
spirit/breath — inner-being SEAT/life-principle operating the inner faculties
spiritual — a quality/category, not a faculty in operation
very compassionate — disposition of deep compassion (affect)
evil/wickedness/malice — moral quality against a standard
evil/wicked/bad — moral quality against a standard
more evil — comparative moral quality against a standard
pain/anguish/toil — felt pain/anguish (affect); also labour
gentleness/meekness — a temper of mildness (affective disposition)
gentle/meek/humble — disposition of mildness (affect)
gentleness/meekness — disposition of mildness (affect)
to decide beforehand/choose deliberately — deliberate choice (volition + prior reasoning)
to foreknow/choose beforehand — knowing in advance (cognition) and elective purpose (volition)
foreknowledge — advance knowing (cognition)
traitor/betrayer — a person/agent
purpose/aim/resolve — settled willed intention
eagerness/readiness/willingness — willing readiness (volition)
eager/willing — willing readiness (volition)
to provide for/take thought beforehand — forethought (cognition) and provident intent (volition)
foresight/provision — forethought (cognition)
to expect/wait for/look for — anticipatory cognition, often with affective foreboding/hope
expectation/foreboding — anticipation (cognition) with affect
prayer — a religious act/practice of address to God
to pray — a religious act of address to God
to call to/summon — a summoning act
stumbling block/offense — a thing/cause of stumbling
stumbling/offense — a thing/occasion of stumbling
to strike against/stumble — a physical/figurative act of stumbling
to worship/bow down — act of reverent homage expressing reverence (affect)
lovely/pleasing — quality evoking affection (affect-related)
to call to/address — a speech/summoning act
to purpose/set forth/intend — willed purpose
prophecy — a charismatic utterance/gift, not an inner faculty in this taxonomy
to prophesy — a charismatic utterance act
to choose/appoint beforehand — elective choice (volition)
firstborn — a status/title
to stumble/fall/fail — a figurative act of failing, not a faculty
to frighten/terrify — fear (affect)
fear/terror — fear (affect)
to be frightened/intimidated — fear (affect)
to become poor — a material state/change
poor/beggarly — a material/social state
to harden (the heart/mind) — induced insensibility of perception/understanding
hardness/hardening — insensibility of perception/understanding
(Lord of) hosts/armies — a title/thing
fleshly/carnal — a quality/orientation, not a faculty in operation
of flesh/fleshly — a material quality
flesh/body — a physical thing/principle (the sinful nature is an orientation, not a discrete faculty)
to worship/be devout/revere — reverent devotion (affect)
dignified/honorable/noble — a character quality/state, not a faculty
dignity/gravity — a character quality/state
to strengthen — an act conferring strength
to be silent/keep silent — a behavioural state of silence
silence — a state
to sift — a figurative testing act (winnowing); not itself a faculty
to be silent/still ('Peace, be still') — a behavioural state of silence
to cause to stumble/offend — an act causing another to fall
stumbling block/snare — a thing/cause of offense
hardness of heart — insensibility/obstinacy: dulled perception and stubborn will
hardness/obstinacy — dulled perception and stubborn will
to trouble/harass/weary — induced distress/weariness (affect)
plunder/spoil — a thing
wisdom — the faculty of skilled understanding (cognition)
to make wise/devise cleverly — cognitive skill/contrivance
wise/skilled — possessing the faculty of wisdom (cognition)
to have/be moved with compassion/pity — felt compassion (affect)
to be eager/diligent/do one's best — willed diligence (volition)
eager/earnest/diligent — willed diligence (volition)
diligence/earnestness/zeal — willed diligence with affective earnestness
groaning — expression of inner distress/longing (affect)
to groan/sigh — expression of inner distress/longing (affect)
to be crushed/distressed/restricted — felt distress (affect); also literal restriction
distress/anguish/calamity — felt distress (affect)
firm/solid/strong — a physical/structural quality
to strengthen/make firm — an act conferring firmness
firmness/steadfastness — a quality/state
to rejoice with/together — shared joy (affect)
to be grieved (with) — grief (affect)
to reign/rule with — an act of co-rule
counsel/plan/plot — deliberation (cognition); also the council body
counselor/adviser — a role/person, not a faculty
to share/share with — a transfer/sharing act
conformed/sharing the form — a state of likeness
to sympathize/have compassion — fellow-feeling (affect)
sympathetic — disposition of fellow-feeling (affect)
to suffer with/together — shared suffering (an experience/state), borders affect
of one accord/harmonious — a relational state of unity
to strive together — striving/struggling act (agency)
fellow servant/slave — a person/role
conscience — the inner moral witness
to be aware/conscious of/know within — inner co-knowing (cognition + conscience)
understanding/discernment/insight — the faculty of comprehension (cognition)
intelligent/understanding/discerning — possessing comprehension (cognition)
to hold/constrain/be hard pressed — figurative inner constraint/distress (affect); also literal hold
to delight in — felt delight (affect)
to understand/comprehend — the faculty of comprehension (cognition); perception of meaning
anguish/distress — felt distress (affect)
to join in hypocrisy/act insincerely — feigning (deceptive self-presentation; engages perception of others/pretense)
to groan together — shared expression of inner distress/longing (affect)
to save/deliver/heal — an act of rescue, not an inner faculty
savior/deliverer — a person/role/title
salvation/deliverance — a state/event, not a faculty
salvation/saving — a state/means
to be of sound mind/self-controlled/sober judgment — self-governed reason and will
self-discipline/self-control/sound mind — self-governed reason and will
self-controlled/sober-minded — self-governed reason and will
lowly/humble/downcast — humble disposition (affect/self-estimation)
humility/lowliness of mind — humble self-estimation (affect + self-regard cognition)
to humble (oneself) — willed self-abasement; humbling act
humiliation/humble estate/lowliness — a state/condition
to dare/be bold — courageous resolve (affect+volition)
bold/daring man — one characterised by bold resolve (affect+volition)
trembling — bodily expression of fear/awe (affect)
blind — a physical/figurative state; the figurative is dullness of perception but the lemma is a state-adjective
to blind/make blind — figurative darkening of perception/understanding
to be conceited/puffed up — affective self-exaltation (pride)
to mistreat/insult/treat shamefully — an act of outrage done to another
to sing praise/hymn — a praise act/utterance
obedience (hearing-under) — attentive hearing (perception) yielding submission (volition)
to obey/heed/answer — attentive hearing (perception) and submission (volition)
to intercede (on behalf of) — an act of pleading/intercession, not a faculty
to be higher/excel/surpass — a comparative quality/status
pride/arrogance — affective self-exaltation (pride)
proud/arrogant/haughty — affective self-exaltation (pride)
authority/eminence/high position — a status/rank
to think too highly/be haughty — inflated self-regard (cognition of self + pride affect)
accountable/liable to judgment — a legal status, not a faculty
to pretend/feign — an act of dissembling (deceptive self-presentation), not itself a faculty
hypocrisy/insincerity — a deceptive disposition/practice, not a discrete faculty
hypocrite — a person/role characterised by pretense, not a faculty
